problem_statement: Static/SAST tools detect vulnerabilities in code but their developer-centric outputs lack regulatory alignment and require extensive manual interpretation, so they fail to produce auditor-ready, standards-mapped evidence; single-LLM approaches add reliability and transparency risks.
magnitude_evidence: Traditional SAST tools identified between 25 and 35 issues on the DVNA repository whereas Code Prism identified 42 issues with audit-ready OWASP/NIST references
insufficiency_evidence: single-LLM approaches tempered by persistent issues of hallucination, inconsistency, and a lack of inherent auditability; SAST outputs 'typically require hours of manual interpretation and formatting into audit-ready evidence'
solution_mechanisms: specialized agents (detection, regulatory mapping, report synthesis, UX)
model selection to validate ambiguous findings
notes: Some garbled OCR ('DICUSSION', 'Figure I.t'); evaluation limited to a single vulnerable repository (DVNA) with mainly qualitative feedback.
